title: Central Diabetes Insipidus
chief_complaint: I'm drinking 15 liters of water a day and peeing every hour, even at night. I can't sleep. I'm thirsty all the time. I had a head injury in a car accident 3 months ago.

physical_exam.Neurologic: Normal. No visual field defects. Fundoscopy normal.
physical_exam.Extremities: No edema. Skin turgor slightly decreased.

hidden_data.urine_osmolality: Urine Osm 80 mOsm/kg (inappropriately dilute - should be concentrated if dehydrated)
hidden_data.water_deprivation: After 8h water deprivation: Serum Osm 320, Urine Osm 120. After DDAVP: Urine Osm increases to 650 (responds to ADH - CENTRAL DI).
hidden_data.mri_brain: Loss of posterior pituitary bright spot on T1. Thickened pituitary stalk. Small lesion in hypothalamus (likely post-traumatic scarring).

key_learning_points: DI = inability to concentrate urine due to ADH deficiency (Central) or ADH resistance (Nephrogenic). Results in polyuria (>3L/day), polydipsia, and hypernatremia
Central DI: Brain trauma, surgery, tumor, infiltrative disease, idiopathic. Nephrogenic DI: Lithium, hypercalcemia, hypokalemia, genetic (AVPR2 mutation)
Water deprivation test: If urine Osm <300 after deprivation, give DDAVP. If urine Osm rises >50% = Central DI. If no response = Nephrogenic DI
Treatment: DDAVP (Desmopressin) 10mcg intranasal or 0.2mg PO BID. Allows kidneys to concentrate urine. Must allow periodic breakthrough to prevent hyponatremia
Danger: If patient continues drinking excessively while on DDAVP → hyponatremia from water intoxication. Educate about thirst management
